preamble: A shift from the tip of the tongue (dental/alveolar) to the back (velar) is relatively rare but well-documented in specific environments, often driven by place assimilation or specific historical sound laws.

examples.note: Proto-Athabaskan 'wife' shifted syllable-final d to g
Proto-Athabaskan 'smoke' shifted syllable-final d to g
Borrowed from Swahili; alveolar d replaced by velar g

sources: Bantu Phonology: Loanword Adaptation in Shambaa
